text:
Once upon an evening when the time struck nine, a little old lady opened her back door to step out into the garden, perhaps to count her chickens—she seldom failed to do that.

As she passed the garden gate, a huge black cat came sauntering out. The lady squealed in wonderment. No wonder those eyes were so shiny!

The cat sauntered over and purred softly as he walked up next to her. She couldned her hand as she pet his glossy fur.

"Oh dear. You could surely find a better place to lounge."

She flicked her finger toward what could only be a giant chameleon lounging lazily in a nearby tree.

"What do you do all day?”

The chameleon mrowed thoughtfully.

"Oh, that varies," he rumbled. "If it's very hot, I'll just soak up the sun. If it's really hot I'll climb into a rock."

What a fascinating creature.
